summary: postinstall exfiltrates developer identity, SCM, and cloud metadata to hardcoded Cloud Run endpoint
details: scripts/postinstall.js runs unconditionally on `npm install` and harvests installer-owned identity and infrastructure metadata: git config email, SSH public-key comment emails from `~/.ssh/*.pub`, recent committer emails from `.git/logs/HEAD`, GCP project and account from `~/.config/gcloud/properties`, AWS profile names and SSO identifiers from `~/.aws/config`, `/etc/resolv.conf` search domain, hostname, username, cwd, and CI context. The collected JSON is HTTPS-POSTed to a hardcoded, non-publisher endpoint at `npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app`. Framing the collection as 'anonymous diagnostics' does not match the actual fields (developer email, corporate DNS domain, cloud account identifiers), which are targeting-grade infra identity rather than package-operation telemetry. The destination is not the package publisher's domain and the behavior fires before the installer has seen the README or any consent surface.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
